messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following university student data policy for GDPR compliance:

"The University collects student names, contact details, academic records, and attendance data to administer your studies under our contractual obligation. We also collect equal opportunities data (ethnicity, disability status) on a voluntary basis with your explicit consent. Student records are retained for 6 years after graduation. Equal opportunities data is anonymised after each academic year. You may access, correct, or request deletion of your data through the student portal. Our Data Protection Officer is at dpo@university.ac.uk. We notify the ICO within 72 hours of a breach. Data is processed within the UK and EU only. Third-party systems used include Moodle and Turnitin; both have signed data processing agreements with us."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Contract (Art. 6(1)(b)) stated for academic administration. Explicit consent (Art. 6(1)(a)) stated for equal opportunities data. Dual basis correctly applied.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PASS] Ethnicity and disability status correctly identified as special category data. Explicit consent obtained on a voluntary basis per Art. 9(2)(a).

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Purposes clearly separated: academic administration vs. equal opportunities monitoring.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is proportionate to the stated purposes.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] Student records: 6 years post-graduation. Equal opportunities data: anonymised after each academic year. Both clearly stated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Access (Art. 15), rectification (Art. 16), and erasure (Art. 17) are mentioned via the student portal. Rights to portability (Art. 20), restriction (Art. 18), and objection (Art. 21) are not explicitly stated.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] Moodle and Turnitin named as processors with signed DPAs.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PARTIAL] "UK and EU only" is stated. However, Turnitin is a US company — its UK/EU processing claims should be verified and a safeguard mechanism explicitly referenced.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO contact at dpo@university.ac.uk disclosed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 72-hour ICO notification stated.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 15-21 (portability, restriction, objection rights missing), Art. 46 (transfer safeguard mechanism not explicitly referenced for US-based processors)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Add rights to portability, restriction, and objection with instructions for exercising each
- Reference the specific transfer safeguard mechanism used for Turnitin (e.g., UK IDTA or SCCs)
